data.title: Betolar Plc: Business Review for 1-3/2025 will be published on 25 April - Invitation to Webcast
data.description:
Betolar Oyj

Betolar Plc

Press Release

16 April 2025 at 9.45 A.M. EEST

Betolar Plc: Business Review for 1–3/2025 will be published on 25 April – Invitation to Webcast

Betolar will publish its Business Review for January-March 2025 on Friday 25 April 2025, approximately at 8.30 Finnish time. Betolar will host a live webcast in English for investors and media on the same day at 10 A.M. Finnish time. The webcast can be followed at https://betolar.events.inderes.com/q1-2025

During the webcast, President & CEO Tuija Kalpala and CFO Mikko Wirkkala will present Betolar's Business Review and other topical issues. The recording and presentation materials will be available on Betolar's website after the event at www.betolar.com/reports-and-presentations

Betolar Plc

For more information:

Mikko Wirkkala, CFO, Betolar Oyj, mikko.wirkkala@betolar.com, tel. 0504584190

About Betolar

Betolar is a circular economy enabler and materials technology specialist, providing innovative solutions to help use industrial sidestreams to produce low-carbon and cement-free products for the mining and construction sectors.  Betolar's mission is to help reduce carbon emissions and the use of virgin resources in mining and construction. 

Betolar's circular economic innovations significantly reduce CO2 emissions from cement use and the use of virgin raw materials by utilizing industrial sidestreams.  Betolar is continuously developing its low-carbon Geoprime solution for the mining, metals and construction industries. In addition, Betolar's AI-based data platform creates value from industrial side streams and accelerates solution development. The SidePrime analytics service, based on the data platform, maps the potential for utilization of industrial sidestreams and waste. 

Betolar was founded in 2016 and is domiciled in Kannonkoski, Finland. Betolar is listed on the Nasdaq First North Growth Market. For more information www.betolar.com

https://news.cision.com/betolar-oyj/r/betolar-plc--business-review-for-1-3-2025-will-be-published-on-25-april---invitation-to-webcast%2Cc4136287

data.title: REG-Reporting of trading in Tryg shares by senior management
data.description:
Group CEO Johan Kirstein Brammer has sold 32,500 Tryg shares at DKK 149.97 for a total amount of DKK 4,873,917 on 14 April 2025. 

Group COO Lars Bonde has sold 32,500 Tryg shares at DKK 149.72 for a total amount of DKK 4,865,786 on 14 April 2025.

Referencing company announcement no. 08/2025 dated 29 January 2025, both individuals have thus net added to their holding of Tryg shares during 2025.

Attachment

  • 22_2025.04.16 Transaction statement

OFFEROR: Dar Al-Handasah Consultants Shair and Partners Holdings Ltd ("Sidara")

Offeror identified: 14:31 24-Feb-2025

Rule 2.6 deadline: 17:00 17-Apr-2025

Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required

Notes:

1.

Rule 8 of the Takeover Code (the "Code") sets out the circumstances in which Dealing Disclosures and/or Opening Position Disclosures are required to be made by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them, persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more and exempt principal traders ("EPTs") connected with the parties to an offer.

2.

Where an offeror is marked "Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required", dealings and positions in relevant securities of that offeror are not required to be disclosed under Rule 8. This is either because it has been announced that the offer or possible offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash or because disclosure is not required for some other reason.

3.

Dealings and positions required to be disclosed under Rule 8 of the Code include dealings and positions in derivatives referenced to, and options in respect of, relevant securities of an offeree company and, where appropriate, an offeror (see Note 2 above).

4.

The information set out in the Disclosure Table relating to the relevant securities in issue of an offeree company or of an offeror (and any amendments thereto) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by such company under Rule 2.9 of the Code. Similarly, details of any deadline set under Rule 2.6 by which a potential offeror must announce either a firm intention to make a bid or that it does not intend to make an offer (or, in the case of Rule 2.6(a), by which the Panel must consent to an extension of the deadline) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by the offeree company.

5.

Additions and deletions of offeree companies and offerors are reflected in the Disclosure Table throughout the day, following the relevant announcement. Other amendments may also be made throughout the day. In any event, details of the classes of relevant securities issued by an offeree or offeror company, the number of such securities in issue ("NSI") and the International Securities Identification Number ("ISIN") of such securities announced under Rule 2.9 of the Code before 5.00 pm each day will be included in the final version of the Disclosure Table by 5.30 pm that day. The final version of the Disclosure Table issued each day will be marked "Final" and persons required to make Dealing Disclosures and Opening Position Disclosures are advised to use that final version. Any changes to the Rule 2.6 deadline of a potential offeror will also be reflected in the "Final" version of the Disclosure Table, although any such changes will not be shown in the "Amendments" section at the top of the Disclosure Table.

6.

Dealing Disclosures by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them and connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the business day (as defined in the Code) following the date of the relevant dealing and Dealing Disclosures by persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by 3.30 pm on the business day following the date of the relevant dealing.

7.

Opening Position Disclosures by the parties to an offer and by connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Where required, Opening Position Disclosures by persons with interests in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by no later than 3.30 pm on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Details of the time and date at which an offer period commenced and at which an offeror was first identified are set out in the Disclosure Table. In order to calculate the deadline for Opening Position Disclosures, it is therefore necessary to count forward 10 business days from the relevant date.

8.

Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service ("RIS").

9.

This Note is applicable if it says "See Note 9 below" by the name of the company in the Disclosure Table above.  The Executive has granted the company a dispensation from the requirements under the Code that announcements must be published via a RIS. The company is instead required to publish all announcements on its website. The Executive has also granted a dispensation from Note 3 on Rule 8 of the Code that disclosures made under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a RIS. Therefore, Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code may be made to the company at the address stated in the announcement that commenced the offer period.

10.

The following are approved Regulatory Information Services:

data.title: Change in Kamux Group Management Team: Jani Koivu, Managing Director of Kamux Finland leaves his position, Joni Tuominen appointed as acting Managing Director
data.description:
Kamux Corporation | Stock Exchange Release | April 16, 2025 at 09:45:00 EEST

Kamux Corporation and Jani Koivu, Managing Director of Kamux Finland, have agreed today that Koivu will leave his position in the company. Koivu has worked as Managing Director of Kamux Finland and a member of the Group Management Team since 2022. Joni Tuominen (MSc Economics) has been appointed acting Managing Director of Kamux Finland as of April 16, 2025. Tuominen has worked as Chief Operating Officer and Development Officer of Kamux Finland as of January 1, 2025.

CEO Tapio Pajuharju: ”I would like to use the opportunity to warmly thank Jani Koivu for his significant contribution to Kamux. I am very pleased to have Joni Tuominen to take the helm at Kamux Finland.”

Following the aforementioned change, the members of the Group Management Team are:

Tapio Pajuharju, CEO;

Jukka Havia, CFO (until early May, 2025);

Juha Kalliokoski, COO;

Johan Kempas, Managing Director, Kamux Sweden;

Joni Tuominen, acting Managing Director, Kamux Finland;

Martin Verrelli, Managing Director, Kamux Germany;

Joanna Clark, Chief People Officer (as of May 15, 2025);

Aino Hökeberg, Chief Marketing and Concept Officer;

Jarkko Lehtismäki, Chief Digital Officer; and

Altti Väisänen, Director, Business Development, Car Flow & Data.

For more information, please contact:

Tapio Pajuharju. CEO, tel. +358 50 577 4200

Kamux Corporation

Communications

Kamux is a retail chain specialized in used cars and related integrated services. Kamux combines online shopping with an extensive showroom network to provide its customers with a great service experience anytime, anywhere. In addition to digital channels, the company has a total of 68 car showrooms in Finland, Sweden and Germany. Since its founding in Hämeenlinna, Finland, in 2003 the company has sold almost 600,000 used cars, 66,548 of which were sold in 2024. Kamux’s revenue in 2024 was EUR 1,010 million and its average number of employees was 904 in terms of full-time equivalent employees. Kamux Corporation is listed on Nasdaq Helsinki Ltd. For more information, please visit www.kamux.com

data.title: Wall Street Journal - April 16
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Wall Street Journal. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- Meta Platforms saw CEO Mark Zuckerberg offer $450 million in late March to settle an FTC antitrust case set for trial, a fraction of the $30 billion demanded, with Zuckerberg expressing confidence in support from President Trump.

- Nvidia expects a charge of up to $5.5 billion linked to exporting its AI H20 chips to China, impacting its first-quarter results for the period ending April 27, due to inventory, purchase commitments, and related reserves.

- The Trump administration plans to use tariff negotiations to press U.S. trading partners to restrict dealings with China, seeking commitments from over 70 nations to block Chinese goods transshipments, exclude Chinese firms, and shun cheap industrial products for reduced U.S. tariffs.

- H Partners, a 9% shareholder in Harley-Davidson is gearing up to challenge the motorcycle maker's board, pushing to oust three veteran directors and replace the CEO swiftly at the mid-May annual meeting amid slumping sales.

- President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Tuesday aimed at reducing prescription drug costs for many Americans, including Medicare recipients.

- President Donald Trump appointed Internal Revenue Service (IRS) criminal investigator Gary Shapley, who previously criticized the government's handling of Hunter Biden's tax filings, as the agency's acting commissioner.

data.title: UK's Bunzl Posts Higher Q1 Revenue; Outlook Lowered
data.description:
Bunzl said Wednesday that group revenue increased year over year for the first quarter of 2025.

The British distribution company's group revenue for the period since Dec. 31, 2024, jumped 2.6% at constant exchange rates and 0.8% at actual exchange rates. However, underlying revenue fell 0.9% year over year.

For 2025, the company now anticipates "moderate" revenue growth at constant exchange rates and a broadly flat underlying revenue. It previously forecast "robust" revenue growth and slight underlying revenue growth for the year.

data.title: Nikkei Falls 1.0%, Dragged by Chip, Electronics StocksMarket Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks ended lower as concerns about U.S. tariffs and their economic implications persisted. Chip and electronics stocks led the losses. Advantest dropped 6.5% and Renesas Electronics lost 4.2% after ASML's disappointing first-quarter orders. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.0% to 33920.40. USD/JPY was at 142.39, down from 143.22 as of Tuesday 5 p.m. Eastern time. Developments related to U.S. tariffs remain in focus. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield fell 8 basis points to 1.285%. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0639 GMT - Japanese stocks ended lower as concerns about U.S. tariffs and their economic implications persisted. Chip and electronics stocks led the losses. Advantest dropped 6.5% and Renesas Electronics lost 4.2% after ASML's disappointing first-quarter orders. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.0% to 33920.40. USD/JPY was at 142.39, down from 143.22 as of Tuesday 5 p.m. Eastern time. Developments related to U.S. tariffs remain in focus. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield fell 8 basis points to 1.285%. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0636 GMT - ASML Holding's guidance for the current quarter is below market expectations, Citi analysts write in a research note. The Dutch semiconductor-equipment maker expects sales between 7.2 billion and 7.7 billion euros and a gross margin between 50% and 53%, a range it said was larger than usual due to uncertainty around tariffs. The sales guidance midpoint of 7.45 billion euros is below consensus of 7.77 billion euros, the analysts note. Meanwhile, the gross margin midpoint is about 91 basis points below consensus of 52.4%, they add. For the year, ASML continues to expect between 30 billion and 35 billion euros in sales. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0616 GMT - ASML Holding is grappling with uncertainty stemming from U.S. President Trump's fast-changing tariff decisions, Citi analysts write in a note to clients. "Tariff uncertainty is clearly increasing and clouding the outlook," they say. The Dutch semiconductor-equipment maker backed its 2025 targets, but Chief Executive Christophe Fouquet said uncertainty with some customers could lead the company to the lower end of its sales guidance range. ASML is forecasting between 30 billion and 35 billion euros in sales this year. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0601 GMT - Delivery Hero will likely see growth in all regions, excluding Asia, for its first quarter, Citi analysts write in a note. The U.S. bank expects the German food-delivery company to report 8% growth in gross merchandise value, ahead of consensus of 7%. As a result, Citi upgrades its stock recommendation to neutral from sell. It cuts its target price to 23.5 euros from 26 euros to reflect foreign exchange rate fluctuations and headwinds related to the share price of Middle East subsidiary Talabat. Shares closed at 23.44 euros on Tuesday.(najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0558 GMT - ISOTeam's profit margins are likely to improve on usage of drones and the execution of input cost-adjusted pricing on contracts secured post-pandemic, CGS International analysts say in a research report. The drones could reduce painting-related costs by estimated 30%-40% and increase productivity, the analysts say. The brokerage forecasts the facilities maintenance specialist's gross profit margin at 18%-20% for FY 2025-2027 versus 15.5% in FY 2024, as its façade-painting drone and indoor painting robot are likely to be ready for internal deployment in 1H FY 2026. The brokerage initiates coverage of the stock with an add rating and a target price of S$0.096. Shares are 2.9% higher at S$0.071. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Skygate Solutions To Buy Shares in Leader Range Technology for MYR10.7 Million
data.description:
Skygate Solutions agreed to acquire 255,003 shares and 9.9 million redeemable convertible preference shares in Leader Range Technology for 10.7 million ringgit.

The shares will be sold to Ong Chee Fui, Kumpulan Modal Perdana, E&E Catalyst, and E&E Catalyst II, according to a Tuesday Malaysian bourse filing.

Upon completion, Skygate Solutions will hold a 51% equity interest in Leader Range Technology.

The company will fund the deal via internal resources, the investment manager and property developer said.

data.title: Spanish stocks - Factors to watch on April 16
data.description:
The following Spanish stocks may be affected by newspaper reports and other factors on Wednesday. Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy:

BBVA

BBVA has hired Deloitte to prepare the integration of smaller rival Sabadell , which indicates it is confident its hostile takeover will succeed, Bloomberg reported on Tuesday, citing unidentified people with knowledge of the matter.

ACCIONA

Ifema Madrid and the consortium formed by Acciona (60%) and Eiffage (40%) signed a contract to build Madrid's 111 million euro ($126.27 million) new Formula 1 circuit, Acciona said on Tuesday.

DURO FELGUERA

Duro Felguera signed a memorandum of understanding with Algerian Sonelgaz for an amicable termination of the Djelfa Power Plant contract commissioned to Duro Felguera in 2014, putting an end to all claims and litigation between both companies, Duro Felguera said on Wednesday.

data.title: Sartorius up 9% in early trade on Q1 results beat
data.description:
** Sartorius' stock rises 9% in early Frankfurt trade after the pharmaceutical equipment supplier posted Q1 results above market expectations

** Underlying EBITDA came in at 263 million euros ($298.98 million), above analysts' estimates of 234.4 million euros according to a consensus compiled by Vara Research

** The group's book-to-bill ratio came in well above 1, it added, which should reassure the market of a continued recovery, analysts at Jefferies say

** Given the strong results "we expect the shares to outperform by at least 10% today,", analysts at J.P.Morgan say

($1 = 0.8797 euros)

data.title: Maersk Shares Look Attractive at Current LevelsMarket Talk
data.description: Maersk shares are attractively valued despite consensus assuming unprecedented negativity in the ocean unit, Citi analysts write. The stock is cheap on many valuation metrics, and while it's not cheap using the P/E measure, this is because the outlook for ocean is challenging, it adds. In Citi's view, the market may be overlooking Maersk swinging from net debt of $12.5 billion at the end of 2019 to net cash of $6.4 billion at the end of 2024. The market is concerned about trade demand and excess capacity, but Citi notes the potential for higher levels of vessel scrapping and idling and greater responsiveness to challenging conditions. Citi upgrades Maersk to neutral from sell and raises its target price to 11,093 Danish kroner from 9,500 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: China's Domestic Auto Market Will Continue to Outshine in 2025Market Talk
data.description: China's domestic market for autos will continue to outshine this year, thanks to an effective government stimulus, Daiwa analysts write in a note. The Shanghai Auto Show in late April is a near-term catalyst, they say. Daiwa revises up its China EV outlook this year, driven by stronger-than-expected battery EV demand. Daiwa now expects China's EV market to grow 33% on year. The current U.S. tariffs on auto imports from China remain applicable, they say. Some China's auto component names may benefit from the potential pause of a 25% tariff on the auto industry in the near term, Daiwa says. The brokerage expects limited impact from tariffs on Chinese automakers as most have already given up their U.S. market since China-U.S. trade war started. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Hays Posts Decline in Fiscal Q3 Group Net Fees
data.description:
Hays said Wednesday that group net fees decreased year over year for the three months ended March 30.

The British recruitment and human resources company's group net fees fell 11% on an actual basis and 9% on a like-for-like basis.

data.title: Montenegrin hotel operator Fjord swings to loss in 2024
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), April 16 (SeeNews) - Montenegrin hotel operator HP Fjord [MNG:FJOK] said it turned to a net loss of 190,000 euro ($215,000) last year from a net profit of 135,000 euro in 2023 despite higher revenue.

Net sales revenue rose to 3.74 million euro last year from 3.44 million euro in 2023, whereas other operating revenue increased to 624,000 euro from 83,000 euro in the period under review, the company said in a filing with the Montenegro Stock Exchange earlier this month.

Operating costs also rose, to 2.19 million euro in 2024 from 1.93 million euro the year before, while costs for workers' wages and benefits went up to 1.56 million euro from 1.28 million euro. The operating profit thus increased to 534,000 euro from 256,000 euro in 2023, while the after-tax profit went up to 345,000 euro from 187,000 euro.

However, the company reported its net loss from changes in the evaluation of other items linked with share capital jumped to 537,000 euro last year from 52,000 euro in 2023, which resulted in the final negative outcome.

Podgorica-based firm Alpha Invest controls 94.6% of HP Fjord, while the remainder belongs to a number of minority shareholders, bourse data showed.

HP Fjord was established in 1968 to develop and operate hospitality facilities in the Adriatic municipality of Kotor. It now owns hotel Vardar and Galion restaurant, which were renovated and reopened in 2007, as well as city cafe Dojmi and the renowned Adriatic beach Bajova Kula.

($ = 0.883 euro)

data.title: Polish utility Tauron's full-year net profit falls 48% as revenues drop
data.description:
Polish power utility Tauron on Wednesday posted a 48% fall in its full-year net profit, mainly due to lower revenues from electricity sales.

The results were slightly below the company's preliminary figures published in March.

WHY IT'S IMPORTANT

Tauron, Poland’s second-largest power utility by market value, is under pressure as falling profitability in its coal-fired fleet coincides with a growing shift to renewables.

Coal use has declined as the country works to cut its dependence, according to energy think tank Forum Energii, accounting for about 57.1% of the nation's electricity generation in 2024.

BY THE NUMBERS

The company's net profit reached 585 million zlotys ($154.6 million) in 2024, down from 1.12 billion zlotys a year earlier.

Revenues fell 22% year-on-year to 32.54 billion zlotys, hurt by lower electricity sales. Tauron's retail electricity sales slipped to 26.7 terawatt hours from 30.75 TWh in 2023.

CONTEXT

In December Tauron unveiled a new strategy aimed at resuming dividend payments from 2029 and investing 100 billion zlotys by 2035, with a focus on modernizing distribution infrastructure and expanding renewable energy sources.

The company plans to decommission coal-fired units by 2030, aligning with Poland's efforts to cut carbon emissions and transition to cleaner energy.

($1 = 3.7832 zlotys)

data.title: South Korea's Kospi Ends 1.2% Lower at 2447.43
UK's Hays warns hiring challenges will persist in fiscal 2026
data.description:
British recruiter Hays reported a 9% fall in third-quarter like-for-like net fees on Wednesday and warned hiring market conditions will remain tough in fiscal year 2026 due to Europe's ongoing struggles and an escalating global trade war.

Recruiters, already grappling with challenges in major European economies such as a government change in Germany and rising inflation in the UK, are now facing new hurdles after the U.S. unleashed a tariff onslaught on its trading partners.
